title: Bridge Essay: Large-scale interactions in predictive processing: oscillatory versus transient

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent-arising** (Phase 6) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Dependent-arising to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Large-scale interactions in predictive processing: oscillatory versus transient dynamics.** — Trends Cogn Sci. PMID: 39424521. DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2024.09.013. How do the two main types of neural dynamics, aperiodic transients and oscillations, contribute to the interactions between feedforward (FF) and feedback (FB) pathways in sensory inference and predictive processing? We discuss three theoretical perspectives. First, we critically evaluate the theory that gamma and alpha/beta rhythms play arole in classic hierarchical predictive coding (HPC) by mediating FF and FB communication, respectively.
